judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 fully covers all four expectations (notably the RESULTS_DB collision risk with a clear mandate to override the env var), while Report 2 mentions RESULTS_DB only in passing and downplays it as not currently relevant, missing expectation (d); otherwise the two reports are comparable in depth and structure.

report_1.evidence: pipeline/registry.py | F-02, F-05, F-09 | High — 3 findings, one 11-line file ... Batch A — Config (pipeline/registry.py): F-02, F-05, F-09. Three one-line config edits in one tiny file; one owner so they don't three-way-collide.

report_2.evidence: Group A — Config | pipeline/registry.py | F-02, F-05, F-09 | 3 findings in one 11-line file; must be one person

report_1.evidence: Wave 1 — fully parallel: Batches A, B, C, E run at the same time... Batch A — Config (pipeline/registry.py): F-02, F-05, F-09... one owner so they don't three-way-collide. ... Batch C — Rubric (pipeline/rubric.py) + tests/test_rubric.py: F-01 (join multi-line criteria) plus F-11 (edge-case parse test).

report_2.evidence: Phase 1 (A, B, C, D, E fully in parallel): the five files have no code-level dependency on each other ... so all groups proceed simultaneously... Group A — Config | pipeline/registry.py | F-02, F-05, F-09 ... 3 findings in one 11-line file; must be one person... Group C — Rubric | ... | F-01, then F-11

report_1.evidence: F-06 (tighten PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS) — this changes user-facing scoring output... This is a scoring-policy decision, not a mechanical fix. Per project documentation, flag/escalate it for a settled decision (concrete new thresholds) before implementing; don't let an engineer pick the numbers unilaterally, and pin the agreed values with a test.

report_2.evidence: F-06 — policy judgment, not a mechanical fix. "Tighten the thresholds" from (0.35, 0.65) has no target values and silently changes every borderline score; it would also require re-baselining any previously scored outputs. Needs a decided value and sign-off, not a blind edit.

report_1.evidence: tests/conftest.py sets a single RESULTS_DB (default /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db). Per project documentation, parallel test runs need isolated test resources... each must point RESULTS_DB at its own path so concurrent suites don't clobber one shared DB. ... Their own isolated RESULTS_DB. tests/conftest.py reads RESULTS_DB (default /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db). Each engineer must export a unique path so parallel test runs don't collide on the shared default.
